description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, elevated array of photovoltaic panels supported by a robust metal framework. The solar panels are mounted above a sloped, gridded platform that appears to be designed for optimal sunlight exposure, with a visible angle of inclination. The structure includes a base platform with a patterned surface, possibly for drainage or maintenance access, and is bordered by solid black edges. There are several vertical and diagonal support beams, likely made of steel or aluminum, providing stability to the elevated solar array. The installation is accompanied by signage and marked zones, suggesting it is part of a managed urban energy site. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and efficient energy capture.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array on a sloped, gridded platform with sturdy metal supports and bordered by solid black edges. The structure is designed for efficient solar energy capture in an urban setting.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sloped, gridded platform and the full width of the solar panel array above.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the supporting structure.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the solar array and the patterned platform surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled supports and the spatial relationship between the platform and the solar panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with steel and aluminum frame supports
attribute_array_configuration: single row of large panels arranged side by side, approximately 4-6 panels in total
